description: Record type-name references as consumer deps -- the second half of noting. Walk every type-position in the indexed surface (@spec args/results, param annotations, extern signatures, @type bodies, x-is-T guards), parse each with the in-house type-expr (.11), and classify each Name against the namespace captured at that declaration point: a NamespaceSymbol::Type binding -> reference_type(module,name,arity) (the TypeName identity), recorded as an upstream dep of the consumer it feeds -- the FunctionId whose contract/body names it, or the TypeName whose @type body names it. An unbound lowercase name is a free type var (not referenced); a builtin is a scalar (not referenced). That recorded reference set IS each consumer later wait-set: DeriveTypeDef (.2) reads a type body refs to know its waits; the re-seated consumers (.4) read a function refs. Add world.reference_type and the consumer->[TypeName] dep store. Nothing resolves to Ty; no type-algebra. Verify: a @spec naming Foo.t records Foo.t as a dep of that function with nothing resolved; a @type body naming Bar.t records it as a dep of that type; a free type var is not referenced.
title: Reference walk: reference_type over type-positions; record consumer type-name deps
